constraints: Read-only tool tier only (tool-policy:leader-coordination / read-write-scoped read side); write_effective is false for this packet.
Must not read/store the provider error body or API key; the adapter deliberately discards the 400 body (adapter_gemini_http.py:176-179), so the 400 sub-reason is not locally observable.
Root cause of an HTTP 400 cannot be proven from local code alone without a live diagnostic call, which is out of scope.
Checker constants are coupled to the model literal: any model-name change MUST update the gemini_api_adapter checker expectations in the SAME write Building to keep checker `--all` EXIT 0.
Recall the write_scope glob trap (directory-form 'support/' excludes children; use 'support/connection/*' style and one path per file).
missing_scope: Whether the 400 was caused specifically by the model id vs another INVALID_ARGUMENT cause is unknown locally (body discarded by design); disambiguation needs a live diagnostic call outside this read-only scope.
Whether gemini-3.5-flash is a currently-served v1beta model id is asserted only from COO-supplied doc evidence, not verified live here.
Whether GEMINI_API_KEY/GOOGLE_API_KEY is present/valid and the project/location/model-access is enabled in the live env — non-local, unknown.
Final write_scope glob literals and the write-capable adapter for the repair Building are the caller's to declare at intake.
Open product choice: pin the preset gemini-broad-review node to model:gemini:gemini-3.5-flash explicitly vs rely on the fallback constant.
not_proven: the local-code root cause of the HTTP 400 — model-drift is the strongest LOCAL candidate but is not proven without a live call; provider/key/location/billing causes are non-local and are not a code defect (recorded here, not as a transition concern, per the kind instruction)
that changing the model to gemini-3.5-flash will make the live lane return 200 — provider behavior is not proven
that gemini-3.5-flash is a valid served v1beta model (relies on supplied doc evidence only)
live four-provider execution, runtime parallel fan-out, credential validity, and provider/local-callable behavior after the adapter exception

proposed_next_work_boundaries.checker_plan: Re-run the gemini_api_adapter checker (registered in agent_axis_behavioral.yaml:22). It deterministically asserts: admission READ+REVIEW/not-write/not-a-CLI; no-key -> clean FileNotFoundError mapped to local_cli_missing with NO subprocess; mocked request endpoint URL + POST + x-goog-api-key (key not in URL) + contents/parts/text body + timeout passthrough; mocked parse into CLI-mirror shape; HTTP-error/timeout/malformed -> clean ValueError. The three model literals (expected_url, api_model_name, build-request) MUST be updated in lockstep so it stays green, and the full checker `--all` must remain EXIT 0.
proposed_next_work_boundaries.decision2_disposition_proposal: PROPOSAL ONLY (caller decides): do BOTH (a) align the gemini-api default+fallback model to gemini-3.5-flash, and (b) add safe HTTPError status-category recording that captures the numeric status WITHOUT storing the body. Rationale: (a) removes the measured local drift; (b) makes any residual non-200 diagnosable next run without a live re-run. 'Neither' is not advisable given the drift is measured, but final disposition is the caller's, not this Agent's.
stop_conditions: Stop if the mocked checker cannot be made green without touching files outside the declared write_scope.
Stop if a model change would require editing checker secret/auth/.git paths or non-connection/non-checker modules.
Stop before any live call that would read/store the provider body or a credential.
verification_plan: Deterministic mocked checker (gemini_api_adapter) green with the new model id in the captured URL.
No-key path still raises clean FileNotFoundError -> local_cli_missing and spawns no subprocess.
Request URL/header/body capture asserts the NEW model in the URL and that the key is not in the URL.

Live four-LLM lane proof rerun expectation: a clean typed adapter-error is ACCEPTABLE if provider/key/location fails; the gate is that model/request-shape drift is gone (URL shows gemini-3.5-flash) and adapter behavior stays clean. A live 200 is NOT required to close the repair.
proposed_next_work_boundaries.write_scope_glob_guidance: Declare narrow file-form globs (e.g. 'support/connection/*', 'support/checkers/lib/*'); fnmatch-probe before declaring; write-capable lane is codex-local (gemini-api is never a write adapter).
